_note: AZA SSP/SAFE program summary data compiled from public sources. Source date: 2026-05-07. Population numbers cite year/source where stated; participating_institutions lists only zoos named in public sources matched against the AZA institution roster. Population figures should be treated as approximate snapshots — SSP populations change continuously.

species.narrative: The African Penguin SSP, established 1995, is managed across roughly 49-51 AZA-accredited facilities. A 2022 Population Analysis & Breeding Transfer Plan showed 1,128 individuals with 98.79% gene diversity. SAFE African Penguin coordinates oil-spill response, artificial nest construction, and field conservation in southern Africa.
participating_institutions: Smithsonian's National Zoo and Conservation Biology Institute

species.captive_population_note: Roughly 350 individuals across approximately 48 AZA institutions (commonly cited; exact AZA figure not publicly stated). Western lowland is the only gorilla subspecies in U.S. zoos.
species.narrative: Gorilla SSP manages western lowland gorillas across ~48 U.S. zoos; 11 zoos are SAFE program partners with another 9 contributing to gorilla conservation outside SAFE. The program has been highly successful at producing healthy social groups and genetically diverse offspring.
participating_institutions: Smithsonian's National Zoo and Conservation Biology Institute
Oklahoma City Zoo and Botanical Garden

species.narrative: Approximately 250 chimpanzees live in AZA-accredited zoos (broader U.S. captive population includes many more in sanctuaries and former research facilities). The SSP, coordinated through Lincoln Park Zoo's Population Management Center, optimizes group composition and genetic diversity.
participating_institutions: Oklahoma City Zoo and Botanical Garden

species.captive_population_note: Sumatran + Bornean orangutans together held across 51 accredited zoos in U.S./Canada/Mexico; both subspecies designated Green SSP with stable populations. Sumatrans retain ~97.7% gene diversity.
species.narrative: The Orangutan SSP serves 51 accredited facilities across North America. Both Bornean and Sumatran populations are designated Green SSP (self-sustaining, stable, with strong genetic profiles). A 1985 moratorium prevents production of hybrids.
participating_institutions: Smithsonian's National Zoo and Conservation Biology Institute

species.narrative: Managed by the Orangutan SSP alongside the Sumatran orangutan; designated Green SSP with retained gene diversity ~97.2%. Cross-species pairings restricted since 1985 moratorium on hybrids.

AZA Elephant TAG/SSP manages roughly 141 Asian elephants across U.S. accredited facilities. A 2024-2026 SAFE Asian Elephant Action Plan governs population sustainability, transfers, and field conservation. Population viability analyses project an 80% decline over the next century without intervention.
participating_institutions: Oklahoma City Zoo and Botanical Garden
Smithsonian's National Zoo and Conservation Biology Institute

species.narrative: Approximately 147 African elephants are managed across AZA-accredited zoos. Sedgwick County Zoo set records with a herd of eight and multiple expected births in 2025. Currently 13 active SSP pregnancies and 13 births in the past two years.

AZA Tiger SSP manages three subspecies — Amur, Malayan, and Sumatran — toward genetic sustainability for 100 years. Amur tiger captive population stood at 117 across AZA zoos as of August 2020. Coordinated by the Felid TAG with the SSP campaign hosted at the Minnesota Zoo.
participating_institutions: Smithsonian's National Zoo and Conservation Biology Institute

species.narrative: Yellow SSP — Felid TAG target population is 150; only 72 individuals across AZA institutions as of 2020. Wild population estimated at fewer than 250 mature individuals.

species.captive_population_note: Roughly 600-700 snow leopards globally in zoos; AZA SSP has built genetic and demographic stability across 70+ North American zoos.
species.narrative: Snow Leopard SSP launched 1984 — among the most mature SSPs. Maintains stable populations across 70+ North American zoos with low difficulty achieving breeding goals.

Red Panda SSP coordinates breeding across roughly 85 AZA-accredited institutions, managing approximately 240 individuals (numbers vary by year). SAFE Red Panda hosted at Pittsburgh Zoo focuses on field conservation in Nepal and Sikkim.

species.captive_population_note: Specific AZA total not publicly stated; SSP redesignated polar bears as a 'research population' rather than 'conservation action' population.
species.narrative: Polar Bear SSP, established 2004 under the Bear TAG, designates AZA polar bears as a research population. Polar Bears International partners with the SSP on training-based studies of physiology, behavior, and reproduction.
Approximately 55 Eastern black rhinos at 27 AZA-accredited zoos. The Rhino TAG manages four rhino subspecies (Eastern black, Southern black, Southern white, Indian/greater one-horned).

White Rhino SSP coordinates breeding to maintain genetic diversity in the AZA southern white population. Major holders include drive-through safari parks. Lion Country Safari alone houses 15 individuals.
participating_institutions: Lee G. Simmons Conservation Park & Wildlife Safari

species.narrative: AZA holds 136 Masai giraffes across 35 institutions (2025). Managed separately under its own SSP from the Generic Giraffe SSP. Antelope/Cattle/Giraffid/Camelid TAG oversees both programs (~500 giraffes total).
Approximately 100 okapis live in AZA-accredited zoos under a cooperatively managed SSP. The U.S. population is paired with the European EEP for global genetic management.

species.captive_population_note: Globally ~1,700+ cheetahs in captivity (2014). U.S. captive population includes ~400 breeding individuals across zoos and sanctuaries (broader than just AZA).
species.narrative: Cheetah SSP works toward a long-term, genetically healthy North American cheetah population. The dedicated cheetahssp.org coordinates breeding centers and partners with field conservation in Africa.
participating_institutions: Smithsonian's National Zoo and Conservation Biology Institute

species.narrative: African Lion SSP, founded 1993, oversees breeding/transfer recommendations for 370+ lions across 105 AZA facilities. Award-winning program; population is at an all-time high inside AZA.
participating_institutions: Smithsonian's National Zoo and Conservation Biology Institute
Oklahoma City Zoo and Botanical Garden

species.narrative: Recovered from a low of 24 individuals in the 1980s. SSP partnership with USFWS produced 11,000+ ferrets in 30+ years; ~300 in managed care today across six breeding facilities, with ~100 released annually.
participating_institutions: Smithsonian's National Zoo and Conservation Biology Institute

species.narrative: Approximately 280 red wolves across 44 SAFE facilities (2025). 26 pups survived from 12 litters in 2024-2025 breeding season. SSP/SAFE serves as genetic reservoir and reintroduction source for the eastern North Carolina recovery population.

Mexican Wolf SSP coordinates ~350 wolves across roughly 60 facilities in U.S. and Mexico, managed as a single metapopulation. Reintroduction supports the Blue Range wild population, which has rebounded from extirpation.
participating_institutions: The Living Desert Zoo and Gardens

species.narrative: 163 whooping cranes in human care; 51 females recommended for breeding at 8 institutions. Total wild + captive population ~600. Captive flock plagued by reproductive challenges including low fertility.
participating_institutions: Smithsonian's National Zoo and Conservation Biology Institute

species.narrative: 192 wild and 177 captive birds as of March 2024 (USFWS). Captive breeding occurs at Los Angeles Zoo, San Diego Zoo Safari Park, Oregon Zoo, the Peregrine Fund's World Center for Birds of Prey, and Chapultepec Zoo. HPAI outbreak halted releases in 2023.

In 2023 the Bonobo SSP transitioned to a Bonobo Management Partnership chaired by Columbus Zoo. Approximately 85 bonobos across 7 U.S. zoological institutions plus one in Japan; ~120 additional individuals in European zoos.
Jaguar SSP coordinates breeding and transfers across AZA institutions (specific AZA totals not publicly stated). Recent SSP transfers include Birmingham Zoo to Florida zoo (2025).
Maned Wolf SSP coordinates a cooperative breeding program across AZA-accredited zoos. SAFE Maned Wolf engages government and private stakeholders for South American grassland conservation.
participating_institutions: Smithsonian's National Zoo and Conservation Biology Institute
Oklahoma City Zoo and Botanical Garden

species.narrative: 33 Andean bears in AZA zoos; 8 cubs born across AZA facilities in the past two years. Sustainability remains a challenge given small founder base. SSP coordinator Sara Colandrea also serves as studbook keeper.
participating_institutions: Smithsonian's National Zoo and Conservation Biology Institute

species.narrative: 42 sloth bears at 15 AZA-accredited zoos. SAFE Sloth Bear focuses on knowledge transfer and stakeholder engagement to stabilize wild populations. Smithsonian's National Zoo had its first cubs in 12 years in December 2025.
participating_institutions: Smithsonian's National Zoo and Conservation Biology Institute

species.captive_population_note: Yellow SSP. Global captive population (all regions) >700 individuals.
species.narrative: Yellow SSP — population >50 but cannot maintain 90% gene diversity for 100 years. SAFE African Painted Dog targets conservation in West/Central, East, and Southern Africa.
participating_institutions: Oklahoma City Zoo and Botanical Garden

species.narrative: One of the earliest reptile SSPs (founded 1985 at the Bronx Zoo). 338 individuals at 63 AZA institutions (2018). AZA institutions actively support Madagascar rescue and field conservation.
participating_institutions: Smithsonian's National Zoo and Conservation Biology Institute

species.captive_population_note: Captive flock <200 birds across multiple breeding facilities. ~300 birds released annually.
species.narrative: Captive flocks at Houston Zoo, Caldwell Zoo, Fossil Rim, NASA Johnson Space Center, and Sutton Avian Research Center produced over 450 release birds in 2018. Houston Zoo alone releases 250-300 annually.
SSP approved December 1996. Captive breeding ongoing since 1993 across 6 zoos plus federal hatcheries. Cheyenne Mountain Zoo and Saratoga National Fish Hatchery rear toads for 1-year-old release.
73 thick-billed parrots in 10 AZA-accredited zoos. Sacramento Zoo holds the largest flock (21 birds) and the most successful breeding program; staff there coordinate the SSP and studbook.
